findings.detail: reading.meta.json uses 'reading_minutes' instead of the SKILL-specified 'estimated_read_minutes', and is missing the 'citations_used' array field. The file exists and parses; information is present but under non-canonical keys. No hard fail triggered.

format_discipline.notes: All six required sections present in correct order. No frontmatter. H1 is first non-empty line.

length.notes: 1389 words at 200 wpm = 6.945 min. Within [1200, 1600] words and [6, 8] min.

citations.notes: [1] realpython.com cited multiple times throughout. All three resources in bibliography match inline markers.

no_forward_references.notes: pytest named once as a deferred future tool (mention-and-defer pattern, not taught). No forward-referenced data structures, language features, or concepts beyond the allowed prior-topic set and this topic's own scope.

faithfulness.notes: All 8 sampled assertions trace directly to topic_corpus.md sections 5.1-5.5 and 6-7. No claims introduced beyond corpus content.

beginner_readability.notes: All key terms (one job, code smell, testable unit, refactoring, and-heuristic) defined at first use with bold-term / plain-meaning format. Concrete analogies lead abstractions throughout. No jargon dumps. No hedging language. Active voice, addresses learner as 'you'.

formatting.notes: Paragraphs consistently under 4 sentences. Ordered steps rendered as numbered list. Inline examples in every concept subsection. Tables used for comparisons. Bold definitions applied consistently.

assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs, due-week mentions, or grade weights appear in the reading.

scope.notes: Reading teaches exactly: one-job principle, 'and' naming heuristic, code smell, testable unit, refactoring. pytest is named-and-deferred in one sentence only, not bolded or defined. No scope creep.

gold_example_comparison.notes: No gold example exists for topic 12.3. Checked against 2-1 and 3-4 gold examples for tone/format bar — this reading meets that bar: identical section structure, same concrete-first style, comparable formatting density.

summary: Reading passes all checks — correct format, 1389 words within budget, primary resource cited, all terms defined at first use, no forward references, no scope creep, full faithfulness to corpus.
